subject: Re: warp/M in a virtual instance
body:
Hi Pranav,

I don't have any experience with running Warp on a VM, but I know the folks 
at EMBL Heidelberg do it all the time. Though I think their instances run 
on the Nvidia GRID platform, which is probably not what you had in mind. 
Windows guests on a Linux host system with GPU pass-through have also been 
reported to work.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Friday, October 9, 2020 at 2:50:30 PM UTC+2 p.sha...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Dimitry, 
>
> We are exploring the possibility of running Warp/M in a virtual instance 
> of Windows. Do you think that it is possible?
>
> Best,
> Pranav
> --
> Pranav Shah
> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>
> Division of Structural Biology,
> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
> University of Oxford,
> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
> UK
>
